name: Double-Decker Fudge
recipeIngredient: Semi-Sweet Chocolate or Hershey's Mini Chips
recipeInstructions:
1. Line an 8-inch square pan with foil, extending foil over the edges of the pan.
2. Measure peanut butter chips into one medium bowl and chocolate chips into a second medium bowl.
3. In a heavy 3-quart saucepan, combine sugar, marshmallow creme, evaporated milk, and butter.
4. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture boils.
5. Continue boiling and stirring for an additional 5 minutes.
6. Remove from heat; stir in vanilla extract.
7. Stir one-half of the hot mixture (1-1/2 cups) into peanut butter chips until completely melted.
8. Quickly pour this into the prepared pan.
9. Stir the remaining half of the hot mixture into chocolate chips until completely melted.
10. Quickly spread over the top of the peanut butter layer.
11. Cool completely. Remove from the pan and place on a cutting board.
12. Peel off the foil; cut into 1-inch squares.
13. Store tightly covered. Makes about 5 dozen pieces or approximately 2 pounds of candy.
Variations:
- Peanut Butter Fudge: Omit chocolate chips; place 1-2/3 cups (10 oz package) Reese's Peanut Butter Chips in a large bowl. Cook the fudge mixture as directed, then add to the chips, stirring until completely melted. Pour into the prepared pan and cool to room temperature.
- Chocolate Fudge: Omit peanut butter chips; place 2 cups (12 oz package) Hershey's Semi-Sweet Chocolate Chips or Hershey's Mini Chips Semi-Sweet Chocolate in a large bowl. Cook the fudge mixture as directed, then add to the chips, stirring until completely melted. Pour into the prepared pan and cool to room temperature.
